findings.requirement: US-20: No horizontal scrolling; min 200-char terminal; query column truncated first
findings.detail: The user-spec explicitly defines the layout constraint: all 17 columns fit without horizontal scrolling on terminals >=200 chars wide, and the 'query' column is truncated first on narrower terminals. The tech-spec view definition still has 'ColsWidth: map[int]int{}' (empty) and assigns no column widths. No discussion of column sizing or truncation priority exists anywhere in the tech-spec. An agent implementing from this spec has no basis for column sizing and would need to guess, risking a screen that overflows or truncates numeric columns instead of 'query'. Partially mitigated by the note that the existing rendering pipeline handles column alignment — but the pipeline needs ColsWidth populated to enforce any ordering.

findings.requirement: US-18: Aux processes (checkpointer, bgwriter, WAL writer, archiver) not shown
findings.detail: The user-spec calls this out as an explicit constraint with a documented rationale. The tech-spec satisfies it implicitly (aux processes have no row in pg_stat_activity so they never enter the pipeline) but does not document this as a deliberate decision anywhere in the Decisions section. A tech-spec reader cannot distinguish intentional exclusion from an oversight. Low risk to implementation correctness; risk to future reviewers misunderstanding the design.

summary: 21/23 requirements fully covered, 1 partial, 0 missing. Round 2 fixes resolved all 2 critical findings (first-tick rate=0 contradiction, ticks source) and both major findings from round 1 (itv=0 guard, procfs parse error handling). 1 major finding remains: column widths for the 17-column screen are unspecified — ColsWidth map is empty and there is no truncation priority defined for the 'query' column. 1 minor finding persists: aux-process exclusion rationale not carried into Decisions section. No critical findings — status: pass.
